I will not back down.
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120763252/587289518/938567526?refcode=header&noskip=true&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZDM3NzdhYy1mZmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pzSpySopqNEkkfSjLofCSBKYzmSFKcG9wVIKYgUVww8=)
Hi team, Robert Garcia here.
Republicans have made one thing very clear: _They are terrified that we’re telling the truth about the Epstein files._
When my team and I sifted through more than 20,000 Epstein documents, we found three emails they desperately hoped would stay buried — **emails suggesting Donald Trump knew more about Epstein’s abuse than he ever admitted.**
So we released them.
Because survivors deserve transparency.
Because the public deserves answers.
Because justice belongs in daylight — not in a locked drawer.
Republicans didn’t like that.
**They attacked me. They tried to discredit the victims. They accused us of “smearing Trump.” Republicans actually said the “most dangerous place in D.C.” is between me and a TV camera.**
**You know what?**_If exposing corruption and fighting for survivors makes me dangerous — then good._
But this is the part I need your help with: _Trump and his allies are now trying to drown out oversight with lies, distractions, and attacks. And we can’t let them._
My team is pushing for full public release of the Justice Department’s Epstein files — not just the ones Republicans cherry-pick. That fight takes resources, staff, and the ability to withstand non-stop GOP pressure.
**[So I'm asking: Will you chip in $10 or anything you can to keep the pressure on and stay in the fight?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120763254/587289532/789508428?refcode=251206_RG_dangerous-fr-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZDM3NzdhYy1mZmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pzSpySopqNEkkfSjLofCSBKYzmSFKcG9wVIKYgUVww8=)**
[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120763256/587289542/-309737922?refcode=251206_RG_dangerous-fr-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZDM3NzdhYy1mZmQyLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pzSpySopqNEkkfSjLofCSBKYzmSFKcG9wVIKYgUVww8=)
Every day this movement grows, Republicans panic a little more — because they know we’re not following their script.
Let's keep going,
Robert Garcia

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: Everyone is chasing the newest dashboard or shiny feature. | | | | December 07 | | | | | Before the Tech Bubble, There’s the Equity Geyser. | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. body:

It’s Governor JB Pritzker.
I’m emailing you today about New Hampshire’s Senate race because this is serious: **Senate Republicans’ hand-picked candidate, John Sununu, just jumped in to defeat Chris Pappas and flip this open Senate seat red.**
**[Before I go any further, I have to ask: Will you rush $5 or more to elect Chris Pappas and keep New Hampshire blue?](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251206_2_pritzker.tTbCMS&refcode=em20251206-2&t=2&akid=3876%2E582294%2EIEGjF8)**
_Without Chris Pappas winning in New Hampshire, Democrats won’t have a chance at taking back a majority in the Senate._ That’s why this race is being targeted by GOP super PACs and Republicans across the country.
And most recently the NRSC — which pours millions into electing Republicans to the Senate — said they’re “all-in” on John Sununu.
This means New Hampshire will see historic levels of outside spending, and we have to be ready to fight back.
Chris is a proven fighter for working families — and he’s seen tough campaigns like this before. He knows it takes grassroots support to win a race like this. That’s why I’m reaching out to you today.
**[If you’re with me and ready to send Chris Pappas to the Senate, will you chip in $5 or any amount today to help him defend New Hampshire’s seat?](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251206_2_pritzker.tTbCMS&refcode=em20251206-2&t=3&akid=3876%2E582294%2EIEGjF8)**

With gratitude,
JB Pritzker

clean_body: No, that's not a typo | | | | | Willis, No, that’s not a typo. I know $1 might not seem like a lot – and it’s not. But combined with thousands of other South Dakotans chipping in, your $1 has an enormous impact. $1 means fuel for the truck so I can drive the backroads and meet voters face-to-face. $1 lets us invest in the simple digital tools that counter the corporate PAC lies. $1 lets us prove that the poll showing me leading Rounds by 8 points when voters hear my story is real. I’m a retired Lt. Colonel, a JAG officer, and a former Park Ranger running on the Cowboy Code of the West. That means I’m running against a D.C. establishment that is counting on you staying silent. When we win, it will be because grassroots donors like you gave what you could now to elect a true Independent. Thank you for your support. Brian Bengs Independent Candidate for U.S. Senate ** | Paid for by Brian Bengs for South Dakota Brian Bengs for South DakotaPO Box 404Hot Springs, SD 57747United States "Website"

name: The Democratic Party
subject: Tonight
body:
---
Derek, We’re reaching out to ask if you can contribute $7 to the DNC tonight. Not tomorrow, not next week, but **tonight.** Here’s why: Trump and Republicans have abandoned the American people since gaining control of the White House and Congress. In the last few months, they have:
* Destroyed the East Wing to build a $300 million gilded ballroom while costs continue to rise.
* Called affordability a “hoax” while their policies have led to skyrocketing costs, a stagnant job market, and devastating cuts to health care.
* Threatened to withhold SNAP benefits during the government shutdown, leaving millions of families unsure about how they’ll put food on the table.

Trump and his allies are hoping that by overwhelming us with their disastrous agenda, we’ll be too exhausted and demoralized to fight back. But we won’t be. Here at the DNC, we’re committed to investing early and building the long-term infrastructure we’ll need to elect Democrats at every level of government — leaders who don’t sell out their constituents for a pat on the back from Donald Trump. That’s why we’re asking for your help **tonight.** The more contributions that we receive _now_ , before 2026 begins, the earlier we’ll be able to invest in the states and districts we need to win in order to flip Congress next year. [**If electing Democrats who stand their ground and fight back against Trump and his cronies is important to you, please consider making a $7 contribution to the DNC tonight:**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-december-2025?amount=7&refcode=em_20251206_5457_fr&refcode2=5457_22930333_QxHTS6&akid=5457%2E22930333%2EQxHTS6) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-december-2025?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20251206_5457_fr&refcode2=5457_22930333_QxHTS6&akid=5457%2E22930333%2EQxHTS6)**

name: Air Force One Alert
subject: Obama just went NUCLEAR against President Trump
body:
This is truly a first...
| Peter,
---
The _UNTHINKABLE_ just happened...
---
First, Obama crept out of his “retirement” as the midterm elections grow near.
---
And now, he’s gone NUCLEAR against President Trump!
---

name: Deb Haaland
subject: this is why I'm running
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO**
body:
Getting here wasn't easy, friend

| | | [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/134?t=1&akid=6091%2E400544%2EhB6C4B)
---
Friend: In a moment, **[I'm going to ask you to make a $5 donation to my campaign.](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/1586?t=2&akid=6091%2E400544%2EhB6C4B)** But first, will you let me explain who I am, why I'm in this race, and the difference your donation will make?
As the daughter of a Pueblo woman, I was taught the value of hard work from a very young age.

My mother was born and raised in a train boxcar, where the Laguna people built a community far from their home. She proudly served in the U.S. Navy, raised four kids as a military wife, and devoted more than two decades to Indian education at the Bureau of Indian Affairs.

I am blessed to come from generations of resilient and fierce Pueblo women – **and I am the person, mother, and leader I am today because of them.**
[](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/1578?t=3&akid=6091%2E400544%2EhB6C4B)
**But getting here wasn't easy, friend, and the hard work and resilience I learned proved essential.**
Like many New Mexicans, I know what it’s like to struggle. I was a single mom living paycheck to paycheck, relying on government programs like SNAP, WIC, and Medicaid to help keep my child fed and healthy. **Every day, as hard as it was, I had to get back up and keep going.**
But my struggle made me fierce. It fortified my resolve to build a brighter future – not only for my child, _but for every family dreaming of and fighting for a better life._
That's why I decided to run for office in 2018. _And I won._ I became one of the first Native American women elected to Congress, and in 2021, the first Native American cabinet secretary in U.S. history.
**Now, I'm running to be the next governor of New Mexico to help move our state toward a future where everyone can thrive.**
**Every step of my journey has been possible because people like you believed in me,****friend****. So please:[Can I count on you to chip in $5 or more right now to help me win, keep New Mexico blue and deliver progress for working families?](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/1578?t=4&akid=6091%2E400544%2EhB6C4B)**
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Donate $10](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/1650?t=5&akid=6091%2E400544%2EhB6C4B)
---
| | [Donate $35](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/1651?t=6&akid=6091%2E400544%2EhB6C4B)
---
| | [Donate $50](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/1652?t=7&akid=6091%2E400544%2EhB6C4B)
---
| | [Other Amount](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/1578?t=8&akid=6091%2E400544%2EhB6C4B)
---
I know that it takes a village – in life, and in politics – and that's why I'll never stop fighting for our communities.
Thank you for standing with me,
Deb
|
* * *
| | [Donate >>](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/1653?t=9&akid=6091%2E400544%2EhB6C4B)
---
| | [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/1102?t=10&akid=6091%2E400544%2EhB6C4B)
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
[**Receive Fewer Emails**](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails-dh/?&t=11&akid=6091%2E400544%2EhB6C4B)
[**Update Your Information**](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/update-your-information-dh/?&t=12&akid=6091%2E400544%2EhB6C4B)
[**Unsubscribe**](https://act.debhaaland.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-dh/?&t=13&akid=6091%2E400544%2EhB6C4B)
Email us: info@debhaaland.com
**PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO**

clean_body: | ~ | ~ Peter, I had to take a second between acts here at MadSoul Fest to let you know how UNREAL this day has already been!!! I just left the stage and the crowd is HYPE. Everybody is so fired up about the impact each of us can have when we’re registered and actually go out to VOTE. Really, everybody who’s spoken today – Rep. Ayanna Pressley, Manuel Oliver, and more – have made that same impact. We’re energizing a true movement , and when I’ve looked out at everybody in the crowd today, I see the blue wave, live in person. But here’s the best part: It doesn’t stop here. Peter... this is just the beginning. Today’s festival is one step along a YEAR-ROUND journey of organizing, mobilizing, and turnout. Only so many people can attend this event. But if we do this right... the energy we start here can spread NATIONWIDE. ~ If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | ~~ Back to it – more to come!! Thank you!! Maxwell | ~ Maxwell Alejandro Frost is an organizer representing Florida's 10th Congressional District. In 2022, he made history to become the first member of Gen Z elected to Congress. name: Team Griswold
subject: Announcement: EMILYs List Endorsement!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2512-q4-jg?refcode=251206-email [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2512-q4-jg?refcode=251206-email]
Peter –
We’re thrilled to share some big news: EMILYs List has officially endorsed Jena for Attorney General.
EMILYs List is one of the nation’s most powerful organizations supporting pro-choice Democratic women, and their endorsement is a major vote of confidence in the work we’re doing together.
They know that Colorado needs an Attorney General who will:
➡️ Protect reproductive freedom
➡️ Defend our democracy
➡️ Stand up to extremism
As Secretary of State, Jena never backed down from fights that mattered: defending elections, expanding voting access, and protecting Coloradans’ fundamental rights. As Attorney General, she’ll carry that same courage and commitment into the next chapter.
But here’s the truth: this campaign is powered by grassroots supporters like you. To make the most of this major endorsement, we need to show the strength of our movement today. Will you chip in to help us build the momentum this endorsement brings? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2512-q4-jg?refcode=251206-email]
Every donation helps us reach more voters, fight back against right-wing attacks, and run the strongest campaign possible.
Thank you for being part of this campaign and for believing in what we can accomplish together.
– Team Griswold

name: Kerly Dolla
subject: smart toilet seats, mechanical bidet toilet seats
body:
Dear Dpwillis67,

We are a professional supplier of UF toilet seats, PP toilet seats, mechanical and intelligent bidet seat cover from Xiamen(China) which is well-known for its high quality bathroom products. We have been this industry for 20 years. I got your company informaiton from internet and happily found that you are working on the products.

Please kindly allow me to have a brief introduction about our CE certified smart toilet seats as below.
The smart toilet seat has the following features:
* Warm washing
* lady washing+ rear washing.
* Seat heating/non-heating available for option
* Seat cover can be dismantled for washing
* non-electrical washing available if without electricity.
* Waterproof: IPX4
* With CE/CB certificates available.
* With 3 years of warranty.

If you are interested in our products, please feel free to let me know so that I can send you our products in details. .
Best regards
Kerly Dolla
Sales manager
Idealife bathroom

body:

name: Catalina Lauf
subject: I can not stop thinking about what we could do together
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Catalina Lauf for Congress
body:
Everyone says it’s impossible but I hope you’ll at least hear me out

| | | | [](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z1hhsvpj1riddcam695q3t2ce4lrcnd6047467c0_rp22sh2s8i6eor5chg6aoj3c5h2bs)
---

Just think of all the things we could do together, Friend.

With you by my side, we could make a real difference.

Because we both know that actual change happens in the town squares and church halls across America – not at the elitist cocktail parties in DC.

**My name is Catalina Lauf.**

I’m the pro-Trump conservative running in the most hotly-contested race in Florida.

_The RINO establishment is deadset on filling this seat with another puppet who will do the bidding of the ruling class in DC._

But my allegiance is to God, Country, and We the People.

Which is why the Establishment made it their sole focus to demolish me the second I announced my candidacy.

My campaign will be built by the hardworking people who make this country run, and they see our grassroots movement as a direct threat to their regime.

_They despise ordinary people like you and I – and they’ll go to EXTREME lengths to keep us silenced and their grip on power tight._

That’s why I’m reaching out to you today, Friend.

**We need to build a huge wave of support to start this campaign strong and build an early lead.**

The RINO establishment has endless resources to drown us out before we can even get off the ground. We can’t afford to stumble out of the blocks.

So I’m coming to you right now, asking for your support.

**_[Be it $5 or $50, contributions from grassroots patriots like you is the only way we can fight back against the millions they have to defeat us. Can I count on you to chip in?](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zfh1o2g7leoe52soah2pn71a4f0f4g0iddsfoflg_rp22sh2s8i6eor5chg6aoj3c5h2bs)_**
[CONTRIBUTE TODAY](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zpqfg0off4p3osrmprc1ocaldbrh52b5ak1kmig8_rp22sh2s8i6eor5chg6aoj3c5h2bs)
I just announced my run, so these next few hours are everything.

With you by my side, we can build an early lead they can never overcome.

Our victory starts right here – and I’m putting my full faith in you to help make that happen.

My candidacy is in your hands now.

I know you won’t let me down.

[CONTRIBUTE TODAY](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zpmplkf7u1ofmtghk8pmknc5mbvl44hf3cunpmag_rp22sh2s8i6eor5chg6aoj3c5h2bs)
Thank you,

Catalina Lauf
MAGA Conservative

subject: Ziac blood-pressure medication recalled over contamination concerns
body:
A New Jersey drug maker has voluntarily recalled thousands of bottles of a combination high blood pressure medication over concerns the product could be cross-contaminated with another drug, the Federal Drug Administration said. The recall covers more than 11,100 bottles of bisoprolol fumarate and hydrochlorothiazide tablets under the brand name Ziac. ---
Speaker Mike Johnson (R-LA) has had to contend with the rebellion of a handful of House Republican women in recent weeks, with several disagreements spilling into public view. Four Republican women have expressed a wide range of frustrations with the speaker over the last several weeks, including Reps. Anna Paulina Luna (R-FL), Marjorie Taylor Greene(R-GA), [...]...Click to read more

# **[President Trump never underwent an MRI exam](http://trk.libertyledger.)**
---
President Donald Trump did not undergo an MRI during his most recent medical evaluation, despite press reports and the president’s earlier public remark suggesting otherwise. The president misspoke when referencing his diagnostic tests, according to people familiar with the president’s medical records. name: Robert, RSLC HQ
subject: This feels like a punch to the gut
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Republican State Leadership Committee
body:
Here’s one thing you can do to put a smile on my face

Dear Friend,
I'm sitting here at my desk, staring at our membership list, and I'm feeling a little defeated. Your name used to be on this list, but now it's just a memory. Your membership with Republican State Leadership HQ has expired, and I'm left wondering what we did wrong.
I thought we had something special, Friend. I thought we shared a passion for Conservative values and a vision for a better future for our states. But now, it seems like that's all fallen to the wayside...
I know we've reached out to you before, but I wanted to try one more time. I want to believe that you still care about what we're fighting for. I want to believe that you still want to be part of our team.
[If that's true, then please, renew your membership today.](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zdc6tlsco4br11bl4diffombmg2t7d0bqgli70gg_rp22sh2s8i6eor5chg69pr4clh2bs) It's not just about the money; it's about the statement it makes. It's about standing with us against the liberal agenda and fighting for what's right.[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zcrgqaho4402k56mm2vjnk9idiplrbf129kek530_rp22sh2s8i6eor5chg69pr4clh2bs)[RENEW YOUR MEMBERSHIP TODAY >>
](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z3o877h96ohruld4973c8uksfqead59de55i7htg_rp22sh2s8i6eor5chg69pr4clh2bs)I'm not giving up on you, Friend. I'm not giving up on us. But I need you to meet me halfway.
If I don't hear from you, I'll assume the worst... that you've joined the ranks of the Radical Left. I really hope it hasn't come to that.
I want you on our team, Friend.[RENEW YOUR MEMBERSHIP TODAY >>
](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z1qnc5mbtgcqd0ajf7h37so5rvjg73tepr664ibo_rp22sh2s8i6eor5chg69pr4clh2bs)Thank you,
Robert, Republican State Leadership HQ
P.S. I'll be waiting to hear from you.

name: Catalina Lauf
subject: I can not stop thinking about what we could do together
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Catalina Lauf for Congress
body:
Everyone says it’s impossible but I hope you’ll at least hear me out

| | | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zdc7s3dhvf32ckmgb9f1d2veukk5dh88iegvhrl8_rp22sh2s8i6eor5chg69pr4cth2bs)
---

Just think of all the things we could do together, Friend.

With you by my side, we could make a real difference.

Because we both know that actual change happens in the town squares and church halls across America – not at the elitist cocktail parties in DC.

**My name is Catalina Lauf.**

I’m the pro-Trump conservative running in the most hotly-contested race in Florida.

_The RINO establishment is deadset on filling this seat with another puppet who will do the bidding of the ruling class in DC._

But my allegiance is to God, Country, and We the People.

Which is why the Establishment made it their sole focus to demolish me the second I announced my candidacy.

My campaign will be built by the hardworking people who make this country run, and they see our grassroots movement as a direct threat to their regime.

_They despise ordinary people like you and I – and they’ll go to EXTREME lengths to keep us silenced and their grip on power tight._

That’s why I’m reaching out to you today, Friend.

**We need to build a huge wave of support to start this campaign strong and build an early lead.**

The RINO establishment has endless resources to drown us out before we can even get off the ground. We can’t afford to stumble out of the blocks.

So I’m coming to you right now, asking for your support.

**_[Be it $5 or $50, contributions from grassroots patriots like you is the only way we can fight back against the millions they have to defeat us. Can I count on you to chip in?](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z1aljsl4jh5pjig5epelqvm4m8iu8crvim2n0ki8_rp22sh2s8i6eor5chg69pr4cth2bs)_**
[CONTRIBUTE TODAY](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zlhpms0j70mlikeiiimqdjkjf7ccotbqp6imireo_rp22sh2s8i6eor5chg69pr4cth2bs)
I just announced my run, so these next few hours are everything.

With you by my side, we can build an early lead they can never overcome.

Our victory starts right here – and I’m putting my full faith in you to help make that happen.

My candidacy is in your hands now.

I know you won’t let me down.

[CONTRIBUTE TODAY](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z4s6587hfenu5k1d5fe61593q26rsccb0s0di1r0_rp22sh2s8i6eor5chg69pr4cth2bs)
Thank you,

Catalina Lauf
MAGA Conservative

🚨 Marjorie Taylor Greene announced her resignation from Congress effective January 5, 2026.
There will be a special election to replace her.
My name is Shawn Harris. I'm a Democrat, a husband, a father, a Retired Brigadier General, a farmer, and I intend to win that election for Georgia and for the American people.
[If you are in a position to support my campaign, I need you now more than ever. name: Bushra Amiwala
subject: You and I were sold snake oil
body:
| | | | | [](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001GpFsMmeZ1njNO1a5E-0KuY3N5ZgmWUzDewtlJOTfxvm_eRnzeCP47AGHdEzDHcvG0-9GJwkedbcE_jYevBMNwqu-qSK8KvCHgFrk8DUcxSHfiJNb1ZqNCFg7k6N8mvfCw3laE1YbXtJCkHosL4vhvo9Hj0gq3bpJx3fA4pQahDZRptdOG1i823mlYNDcbv1CN954rsUalw0eRjZxXo2G72pTwhJrp0dI&c=h8D1aPcWEArSiaxyplPi3W72ZeQ93cxSB2NGIFiBWersliPqgY0L9Q==&ch=ttmMK6AZt_dzAzHHtdfciaksFWcEUhoxU4LWwPhrjrP_3JbrGMr_cA==)
---
|
Peter,
In 2024, Donald Trump ran on a promise of economic populism, vowing to lower costs and help the working class.
Less than a year later, the verdict is in: Americans were sold snake oil.
---
| [](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001GpFsMmeZ1njNO1a5E-0KuY3N5ZgmWUzDewtlJOTfxvm_eRnzeCP47AGHdEzDHcvG0-9GJwkedbcE_jYevBMNwqu-qSK8KvCHgFrk8DUcxSHfiJNb1ZqNCFg7k6N8mvfCw3laE1YbXtJCkHosL4vhvo9Hj0gq3bpJx3fA4pQahDZRptdOG1i823mlYNDcbv1CN954rsUalw3-4O4sTlA0JfDHZabcGjmb&c=h8D1aPcWEArSiaxyplPi3W72ZeQ93cxSB2NGIFiBWersliPqgY0L9Q==&ch=ttmMK6AZt_dzAzHHtdfciaksFWcEUhoxU4LWwPhrjrP_3JbrGMr_cA==)
---
His policies have pushed the economy to the brink. Energy bills, healthcare premiums, and grocery prices are skyrocketing.
The average US home buyer is now 59 years old. An entire generation is being locked out of the American Dream, saddled with student loan debt and forced to rent forever while private equity firms buy up our neighborhoods.
Seniors can't afford their insulin. Americans are losing their jobs en masse. But billionaires? They're doing better than ever.
This is the consequence of decades of the same old "trickle-down" economics that has rigged the economy to line the pocket of billionaires while families in my district work themselves to the bone just to survive.
It is time for Democrats to actually fix the rigged economy.
That's why I am running on a vision where my neighbors don't just work to survive--but thrive. That means fighting for single-payer universal healthcare, breaking up monopolies that drive costs up, building affordable housing, canceling student debt while making public colleges tuition-free, and cutting our bloated military budget to finally invest in education and our communities.
This is how we fix a rigged system. If you are ready to send a representative to Congress who will bring real solutions to the real problems working people face, I need you with me.
[Can you rush a donation of $25, $10, or whatever you can spare during these troubling times to fuel our movement to fix the rigged economy?](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001GpFsMmeZ1njNO1a5E-0KuY3N5ZgmWUzDewtlJOTfxvm_eRnzeCP47AGHdEzDHcvGZB-p2NL42P5PpEwP6jcp6PSnXf9WgTndls9OQFi6hHaV4L_WOe4nRJRxDOeKQXiF-ZrrzNNm8d7-370WTREnTyGq2X3k3HzbGiCDofRaxfBLCjE8YrvWihsOQMggdhXoDcwpRhTjFVvVkl3n9h-aqm04NJp6WkgLgIgRmjbHzY4=&c=h8D1aPcWEArSiaxyplPi3W72ZeQ93cxSB2NGIFiBWersliPqgY0L9Q==&ch=ttmMK6AZt_dzAzHHtdfciaksFWcEUhoxU4LWwPhrjrP_3JbrGMr_cA==)

name: INGOP
subject: Main Street Momentum
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE
body:
Dear Hoosier Republicans,

No images? Click here https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail19.com/t/y-e-altklkt-hhlljjcji-k/

Main Street Momentum

Dear Hoosier Republicans,

Indiana’s 591,000 small businesses employ more than 1.2 million workers, putting our state among the nation’s leaders in new-business survival and job creation. Coming from Main Street, Governor Braun promised to be the most entrepreneurial governor Indiana's seen, and the results speak for themselves. With the launch of the Office of Entrepreneurship and Innovation, the rollout of Power Up Indiana, and key reforms at the Indiana Economic Development Corporation, we’re attracting higher-wage jobs while cutting the average incentive cost per job from $55,000 to just $16,000. And with Indiana’s AAA credit rating, these reforms are strengthening our economy and keeping the cost of living low at a time when many states are facing uncertainty.

This week, Governor Braun also introduced his new Family First Workplace initiative, allowing eligible state employees to bring their infants to work during the first six months of life. The fight for life is far from over, and a cornerstone of that fight is creating a culture that respects and supports young families.

I am also proud to report that thanks to Attorney General Rokita, Secretary of State Morales, and support from President Trump’s Department of Justice, Indiana secured a major win for election integrity. As of this week, a landmark settlement now forces the federal government to modernize its citizenship verification system, giving every state the tools needed to prevent non-citizens from voting. Early results already confirm illegal registrations and ballots in Indiana, and but this agreement will give every state in the union the tools they need to ensure only Americans cast a ballot on election day.

As always, thank you for being a part of our team, and please feel free to reach out if there's any way I can be of assistance.

Yours in service,

Lana Keesling, Indiana GOP Chairwoman

Governor Braun Announces "Family First Workplace” Policy to Support Working Families

Governor Mike Braun announced the new Family First Workplace initiative, allowing eligible state employees to bring their infants to work for the first six months. Building on his expanded parental leave policies, the program strengthens Indiana’s commitment to supporting young families as they navigate parenthood and careers.

"Indiana is going to lead the nation in pro-family policy. The parental bonding that happens in the first months of a child’s life are irreplaceable, and our new Family First Workplace pilot program helps parents stay connected to their newborns while continuing their careers. Indiana is building a culture that puts families first.”

- Governor Braun via a press release [https://events.in.gov/event/governor-braun-announces-family-first-workplace-policy-to-support-working-families]

Governor Braun Celebrates "Small Business Saturday" in Indiana

With small businesses employing nearly half of all Hoosiers, Governor Braun spent the weekend highlighting their impact through visits across the state. Indiana ranks among the best in the nation for small-business survival and job creation, and Governor Braun used Small Business Saturday to underscore the importance of supporting local shops during the holiday season.

"Indiana's Main Street businesses are the heartbeat of our communities and the engine of our economy. These entrepreneurs take risks, hire local workers, and strengthen the towns and cities they call home. If you're going shopping after your Thanksgiving celebrations this week, I hope you'll join me in shopping local at one of Indiana's small businesses."

- Governor Braun via a press release [https://events.in.gov/event/governor-braun-celebrates-small-business-saturday-in-indiana]

Congress of Counties 2026

Indiana GOP is Hiring Interns for the Spring Semester

📱 News You Can Use

Governor Mike Braun was named [https://x.com/GovBraun/status/1994542573105647924?s=20] one of the nation's most effective lawmakers during his last year in the Senate by the nonpartisan Center for Effective Lawmaking.

Lieutenant Governor Micah Beckwith highlighted [https://x.com/LGMicahBeckwith/status/1995942684608135483] Mayor Yates’ leadership in ensuring Delphi grows responsibly, noting that “growth is good, but not for the sake of growth.” He praised Yates for guiding the community in a way that preserves its character while still moving forward.

Secretary Diego Morales announced [https://events.in.gov/event/secretary-of-state-diego-morales-and-attorney-general-todd-rokita-secure-landmark-settlement-to-safeguard-voter-rolls-identify-illegal-votes?utm_campaign=widget&utm_medium=widget&utm_source=State+of+Indiana] a landmark settlement with the U.S. Department of Homeland Security that will modernize federal citizenship-verification tools and strengthen Indiana’s ability to safeguard voter rolls. Morales emphasized that the agreement, confirming at least 165 non-citizens registered to vote in Indiana, provides long-overdue resources to protect election integrity and ensure every lawful vote counts.

Treasurer Daniel Elliott celebrated [https://x.com/TOS_Elliott/status/1995912812950851787] Giving Tuesday by helping unveil the Giving Machine in Carmel, joined by Mayor Sue Finkam and Governor Mike Braun. He highlighted the event’s message of generosity, noting that 10-year-old Jenna offered a powerful reminder of the importance of giving to others.

Attorney General Todd Rokita announced [https://events.in.gov/event/todd-rokitas-litigation-division-has-achieved-favorable-outcomes-for-hoosier-taxpayers-in-100-of-jury-trials-in-2025?utm_campaign=widget&utm_medium=widget&utm_source=State+of+Indiana] that his Litigation Division achieved favorable outcomes in all 13 jury trials this year, saving Hoosier taxpayers over $1 million and more than $36 million overall in 2025. He credited his litigation team for their strong advocacy and commitment to protecting Indiana’s legal and financial interests.

State Comptroller Elise Nieshalla led [https://www.in.gov/comptroller/files/Indiana-State-Comptroller-Elise-Nieshalla-Leads-National-Call-to-Confront-Americas-Debt-Crisis.pdf] the national call to confront America’s debt crisis, joining 90 state elected officials from across the country as chair of the National Debt Crisis Task Force of state financial officers.

Senator Todd Young introduced [https://www.young.senate.gov/newsroom/press-releases/young-booker-introduce-bill-to-help-american-workers-families-build-emergency-savings/] the bipartisan Emergency Savings Enhancement Act with Senator Cory Booker to help workers and families save for unexpected expenses without dipping into retirement accounts. Young said the bill would strengthen financial security for Hoosiers by expanding access to emergency savings accounts and increasing contribution limits.

Senator Jim Banks noted [https://x.com/SenatorBanks/status/1996573954492797103] that 71% of Americans support striking narcoterrorist boats to stop drugs from harming families. He said that President Trump and Pete Hegseth, Secretary of War, are taking the decisive action the public expects.

Congressman Rudy Yakym voted [https://yakym.house.gov/posts/rep-yakym-votes-for-transparency-trust-and-accountability] for a bipartisan package of bills aimed at improving transparency, accountability, and customer service across federal agencies. He said the measures will better protect families, support identity-theft victims, modernize key services, and ensure Hoosiers receive clear, reliable information from the government.

Congressman Marlin Stutzman joined [https://x.com/RepStutzman/status/1995984822582251825] the Republican Study Committee’s New Media Row to discuss affordability and a conservative path forward on healthcare. He emphasized that Americans want more money in their pockets and said Republican policies are helping make that possible.

Congressman Jim Baird honored [https://x.com/RepJimBaird/status/1996346068527624448?s=20] Gary Steinhardt on the House Floor for his service in the U.S. Army and his work as a professor at Purdue University. He praised Steinhardt’s strong advocacy for veterans through the VFW and American Legion, thanking him for his many years of selfless service.

Congresswoman Victoria Spartz emphasized [https://x.com/RepSpartz/status/1995951941273440614] the need for Congress to codify Association Health Plans and Health Reimbursement Arrangements to make insurance more affordable. She noted Indiana’s alternative approach reduced Obamacare premiums significantly but said lasting solutions require federal action.

Congressman Jefferson Shreve introduced [https://shreve.house.gov/media/press-releases/congressman-shreve-designates-december-4-national-scam-prevention-day] a resolution to designate December 4 as National Scam Prevention Day, highlighting the urgent need to better protect Americans from increasingly sophisticated scams. He emphasized that foreign scam operations pose serious financial threats and said the designation will help raise awareness and give citizens the tools to recognize and avoid costly schemes.

Congressman Mark Messmer invited [https://x.com/RepMessmer/status/1996605264028410069] IN-08 residents to his office’s Constituent Casework Service Meetings, where staff will meet one-on-one to assist with issues involving federal agencies. He encouraged anyone needing help to contact his office for more information.

Congresswoman Erin Houchin reaffirmed [https://x.com/RepHouchin/status/1996335272288760194] her commitment to strengthening kids’ online safety, saying she is motivated both by families who have experienced tragic loss and by the need to prioritize every child’s wellbeing.

Click here Main Street Momentum Dear Hoosier Republicans, Indiana’s 591,000 small businesses employ more than 1.2 million workers, putting our state among the nation’s leaders in new-business survival and job creation. Coming from Main Street, Governor Braun promised to be the most entrepreneurial governor Indiana's seen, and the results speak for themselves. With the launch of the Office of Entrepreneurship and Innovation, the rollout of Power Up Indiana, and key reforms at the Indiana Economic Development Corporation, we’re attracting higher-wage jobs while cutting the average incentive cost per job from $55,000 to just $16,000. And with Indiana’s AAA credit rating, these reforms are strengthening our economy and keeping the cost of living low at a time when many states are facing uncertainty. This week, Governor Braun also introduced his new Family First Workplace initiative, allowing eligible state employees to bring their infants to work during the first six months of life. The fight for life is far from over, and a cornerstone of that fight is creating a culture that respects and supports young families. I am also proud to report that thanks to Attorney General Rokita, Secretary of State Morales, and support from President Trump’s Department of Justice, Indiana secured a major win for election integrity. As of this week, a landmark settlement now forces the federal government to modernize its citizenship verification system, giving every state the tools needed to prevent non-citizens from voting. Early results already confirm illegal registrations and ballots in Indiana, and but this agreement will give every state in the union the tools they need to ensure only Americans cast a ballot on election day. # IN YOUR WEEKLY BULLETIN:
---
|
---
| |
This week’s Sunday Bulletin uncovers a gun-trafficking sentencing, a wave of early-morning car thefts in Abington, and a shocking illegal-dumping bust in a Philadelphia cemetery—plus Ocean City’s bankruptcy bombshell and mounting pressure in Harrisburg over girls’ sports:
**INVESTIGATIVE** : A stunning look inside Philadelphia’s war on illegal dumping reveals broken crypts, exposed remains, and cemetery workers hauled off in cuffs.
**REGIONAL NEWS** : A gun-trafficking middleman is sentenced, a string of Abington car thieves leaves behind body armor and stolen mags, tensions erupt after a Horsham man breaks into a home to “profess love,” and Ocean City’s mayor shocks residents with a bankruptcy announcement.
**SPORTS** : As MLB Winter Meetings heat up, the Phillies brace for the Schwarber sweepstakes and nightmare scenarios involving the Mets—while the Eagles’ embattled offensive coordinator takes the high road after vandals egg his home.
**POLITICS** : Moms for Liberty and other groups push hard to force a vote on protecting girls’ sports, while Delaware County’s tax system comes under fire for what one columnist calls “economic apartheid.”
**EDITORIAL** : Our editors call for an end to America’s gerrymandering arms race—and ask whether Pennsylvania’s leaders are fueling a system rigged against working families.
---

REGIONAL NEWS : A gun-trafficking middleman is sentenced, a string of Abington car thieves leaves behind body armor and stolen mags, tensions erupt after a Horsham man breaks into a home to “profess love,” and Ocean City’s mayor shocks residents with a bankruptcy announcement. SPORTS : As MLB Winter Meetings heat up, the Phillies brace for the Schwarber sweepstakes and nightmare scenarios involving the Mets—while the Eagles’ embattled offensive coordinator takes the high road after vandals egg his home. POLITICS : Moms for Liberty and other groups push hard to force a vote on protecting girls’ sports, while Delaware County’s tax system comes under fire for what one columnist calls “economic apartheid.” EDITORIAL : Our editors call for an end to America’s gerrymandering arms race—and ask whether Pennsylvania’s leaders are fueling a system rigged against working families. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2025 Fideri News.All rights reserved._ You signed up for emails at OnPattison.com Our mailing address is: Fideri News 323 W Front St Fl 2 Media, PA 19063 USA Want to change how you receive these emails? Friend, we won't beat around the bush. This is bad. The GOP has enthusiastically answered Trump's call to take off the gloves and fight Democrats bare-knuckle, no rules and no refs.
And to be honest, right now Trump is winning.
Here's MAGA's scorecard, and things are looking pretty ugly for Democrats:
✅ Completed: Texas. Missouri. North Carolina.
👉 Happening now: Indiana.
🔴 Coming soon: Kansas...and the big one: Florida.
But we can't curl up into a ball and give in to despair. That's what Trump wants us to do.
Instead, Dems need to get up off the mat, brush themselves off, and FIGHT.
Fair Fight Colorado is pushing forward with a ballot initiative that will let Coloradans decide on a new Congressional map to counter MAGA election rigging.
Colorado has four GOP members of Congress, but Republicans haven't won statewide since 2014. Democrats can easily gain two or more seats from redistricting--and can even kick Lauren Boebert out of Congress in the redistricting process.
[The Supreme Court has its thumb on the scale for Trump. Democrats will lose the redistricting wars and possibly the House unless blues states wake up and fight back. subject: Justices Hear Birthright Challenge, Retro Christmas Traditions, and a Christmas Tree Ship
body:
The Supreme Court said Friday it will decide the legality of President Trump's executive order that [seeks to end birthright citizenship](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOhCAURb8GOw0gSKag2MZutt3SoDyGN0Ex8Bwz-_Ubt9kP2O4mN7k353zdp7vDhNvj061gKeRpg7MmIIIyPY9KuECN4MqMhWLBR6QluklyqVshW24m7zC9J9V4GwAk9A1YYQYtuNa9aaIdguOzEUENXM9eG638zYO_aa8EDBAatNeakNwIwXWvu5syg4e5l9y7wIVjioNHyqV7ZtwoQkjv_ILSLXltko1Ee2X9B5Mjk-N5nt0y14vi6pkcr8jkWI-9wArtko9CLZVj3ds_qHZBwm_YasSdybEp1u8npoR1MEzxx-ow_f7RvyS9rPwJAAD__wCaffw), which automatically grants citizenship to nearly everyone born in the U.S.

# Sunday, December 7, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1941, Japan launched a surprise strike on Pearl Harbor, killing about 2,400 Americans and pushing the U.S. into World War II. President Franklin D. Roosevelt called it “a date which will live in infamy.” [Hear Roosevelt’s announcement here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2S3CAQQOHTSBkqQD_sBgROlK1Th6pW0wy9RkLVMKOa27vsK_gCr159v762L-DM5-MnHORbLNtJd83UGsn2_ayNkWoikJ2lJeFHaphgs9rOylil3RaA83ub-uAjkaWxJ2_cMhs9z6Prkw_RzLvGZSK3E-gREQMGNzn3sUyI2LP_WzNWO2P0PM7D5-SWQPtodYCoDXSTpsCtyPBd-GyJYn6XF8mA5eizT61dtRt_dHbt7Hrf93ABcmS8QY5npecxFHl0ds0EcnZ2FarlKUi1s-slVDnQ2VQUOH9nPpWUUulFuSmIjURdBJJVAtmLqHoRYVKBkI6dRH0o8znNvfhw3Zwz18V1k34cwPnfXvsv05e3fwIAAP__i4OPcw). Model trains were once a quaint set-up around the Christmas tree, chugging around metal tracks. But their popularity gave way to remote-control toys and more, as explained in today’s [“Whatever Happened To..”](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDuuIyEQRVfTnWHx6Y8dEIw0cvYmnRBVQzXUEw0WlG159yN7lvDyq3uOzt8v9wWUqcQ_cKDlvbqCz56RGZv7vncmjz0htI0ap0YxsU_gtNSzUFrI1QWg_HLTGOyOqNGMaNW6zErOs1nHZLcz-GDOqHCDy7wuG_jlMiPuu1rUdp5Gsu83peWqlJzNfLpM6xJwM1oG2KWCYZIYiGs7fVcqnHDPr_rAdvL1GLNNzLc-mF-Dvg76uufaKMDH6j0Y9DXVTAFeIlHn2l7imbCIQEHAgY08lC46Q2NxuzNTieKoAbPgBlS62DDXp_CpUecDuuCG2N8sc73z4Xq9N4-D-e0TcLzxf6aJtcaM7kGxvFuOzYbbk3KmvqzDJOMBlD_-_KPoD6v_BQAA__9DPpx7) If you’ve ever wondered what happens when pure passion for craft beer meets a bold vision for redefining the brewpub experience, today's sponsor, [Westbound & Down Brewing Company](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O2zAMgJ_G3hLox7LiQUOBQ7br2lFgJCbiQZYMiU5wb184LfoCXfl9IInv16f_BMpUHj9hRcf36gu-ekZmbP5r70wBe0JoN2qcGj0ShwReCWVOUp2E9REof_tpjO6OqFCP6KSdjRTGaDsmp0FJdZFLmAKAifIyRzkJO-sARkawI7ljm1TCSimMNudlsnPEm1Yiwl1IGCaBkbi281elwgnv-bs-sZ1DXcfsEvPWB_1jUNdBXak8sfP5hZ1vdS8RSoz1VQ73EPR159X3ureAg_7AFSgPaj6GK0ba10F_bEDxtEHjgq0n2v7yAOsG9Chv4x-UF3HSRvs_MfZCDF5fpFZqWpZ5GZuL24typj7bYRKP4-D7cf6v2k-nfgcAAP__gl6UbQ), is the story—and the investment opportunity—you don’t want to miss.
|

## Indiana Beats Ohio State, Duke Upsets Virginia
No. 2 Indiana beat No. 1 Ohio State 13-10, after the Buckeyes missed a 27-yard field goal with 2:48 remaining, securing the Hoosiers’ first outright Big Ten title since 1945. In the SEC, No. 3 Georgia beat No. 9 Alabama 28-7, avenging an earlier loss to the Crimson Tide and claiming its second straight conference title. No. 4 Texas Tech scored 34 unanswered points in a 34-7 victory over No. 11 BYU, securing the Red Raiders’ first Big 12 title and a likely first-round bye in the College Football Playoff. No. 17 Virginia forced overtime in the ACC championship game with a last-minute touchdown pass, but Duke sealed the 27-20 upset with an interception during the Cavaliers’ overtime possession. The College Football Playoff selection show is set for noon ET today on ESPN.
## SCOTUS to Rule on Birthright Citizenship
The Supreme Court said Friday it will decide the legality of President Trump's executive order that [seeks to end birthright citizenship](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOhCAURb8GOw0gSKag2MZutt3SoDyGN0Ex8Bwz-_Ubt9kP2O4mN7k353zdp7vDhNvj061gKeRpg7MmIIIyPY9KuECN4MqMhWLBR6QluklyqVshW24m7zC9J9V4GwAk9A1YYQYtuNa9aaIdguOzEUENXM9eG638zYO_aa8EDBAatNeakNwIwXWvu5syg4e5l9y7wIVjioNHyqV7ZtwoQkjv_ILSLXltko1Ee2X9B5Mjk-N5nt0y14vi6pkcr8jkWI-9wArtko9CLZVj3ds_qHZBwm_YasSdybEp1u8npoR1MEzxx-ow_f7RvyS9rPwJAAD__wCaffw), which automatically grants citizenship to nearly everyone born in the U.S. Under the administration’s plan, [birthright citizenship would be limited ](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOpCAUhZ9GOwygiFNQbGM3225pEK7DnSAYuI6ZffrNTLntX56c5OR835_7crcYMT1-2x0MbXlJcNUIRFCW51kJHdQAtqxYKBR8BHLBLpJLxYRkXC_eYnwvQ-vNBiChb8EIPSrBlep1G4y_KStG4ZWzMDm1cj6t0q6TuHE1DWPfovmsCcm1EFz1qrsNevSw9pJ7u3Fhm4GDR8qle2ZMFGCL7_yC0rm8t9EEoqM2_a9Gzo2cr-vq0uo-FJ--kfORIxK62si5nkeBHZjLZ6H_M_Pg0AOjcu4HczaxiDsSczlVQjoJc7KRfR0wh4R_IdWABysuWTkMmou2GH9cGCPWUTcDf-wW4_cm_cjty8h_AQAA___QFJGl)to those who have at least one parent who is a U.S. citizen or permanent legal resident. The right would not apply to babies born to temporary visitors who entered the country legally or to people who entered the country illegally. Trump signed an [executive order ending birthright citizenship](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OqzAUhJ_GdI78g3EoXNyGLrfdEhl8wCcyGNmHRNmnXyXNPsDWo5nR933dxpvHhPv632_gaMnjDs-agAjKeD8r4Qw1gi8TFooF10hz9KMSynCpuLBj8JheY9sEtwAo0A04aTsjhTHaNtFJqYMX1remlSAWA2bq7BLktRdXWELfoHuvSSWslMJoc-lb2wWYtBLBL0J61goISLlc7hl3irCkV35Aucx5a5KLREdl-h9TA1MDRYiY0jtjaiiwnskT5p2pYc5nIT55ogSVqcF02vRacCrndvBfQD4j4TfsNeLB63kU2IB_ykwNTXHheGJKWDvLWrFuHj9vDf1J38OpnwAAAP__NeWF9A) on the first day of his second term, but courts so far have blocked its implementation.
## List: Top Winter Spots for U.S. Travelers
A new report ranks cities in New York, Nevada, and Florida [among the most popular](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2O6yAURldjd1j8GBMXFK9Jl9dOaWG4DjeDjQU3Qdn9KLOF6T99R-d83Zabw4TH_b_bwdKWlwNaTUAEZXk8K6GHGsGVFQvFgvdIPrpFcqmZkIybJThM72Xsg90AJKgerDCTFlxrZfpozbapdZoufJyCd6ucPazSg1J6msNlnXu0nzchuRGCa6WHeTRTgFVJHtzGhetGDgEpl-GR8aAIW3rnF5TB571PNhKdtVP_Onnt5LW1NnwUGsD3Z9DJ6-5OVmNule25Ejvz-UyusGdlHgmhsoYHQWEv5x1hPioTQshx1KovNpwNU8I6mW7k991h-sXSn1q9rPwJAAD__y1Lf38) in the U.S. for domestic winter vacationers. Data collected from consumers rated destinations on activities, experiences, and budget, identifying New York City, Las Vegas, Orlando, and Key West as top winter spots for those leaving their passports at home. While stateside travelers were split between cold retreats and warm escapes, [international travelers](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkb2u2zAMRp_G3mjox7LTwUOXbLdrR0OR6IgXimRQbNT79kWC9gk6Ejw4wMH382P_8JSp3H_4B25y1L1gbxlFkPfPX00oYEvo-UYsiemeJCS_G2UcaANq3aOn_LXPY9wORIN2xE2vi9PKObuOaZuP4NSiTQg2KmsXvIRV2zl4fXHrzcWRtpdNG7VqrZx107d5XSLerFHRH0r7YVYYSSpPn5WKJDzyV30iT6E-xrwlkbMN9vtgroO59t6nk18RnRhfyGCurxMYM_qGbTBXYTp9fFKrDJ2KIIOwf2IGKhF_A-NZWaCl2tvfD3IDzwiJRKjcQRICVx9BErV_kk6SYFFwZl_Km2I6G1hlFuOUuUxJHnnkLZ6dcqa2rMOs7g9P-d0i_zXAczN_AgAA__84vp77) favored Cancun, Playa del Carmen, Cabo San Lucas, and Bavaro for dependable sunshine and beaches. The study also revealed that London, Bangkok, Cancun, and Paris were the preferred destinations for non U.S. residents.

**Whatever happened to model trains?** If you grew up with a model train [circling the Christmas tree](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDuuIyEQRVfTnWHx6Y8dEIw0cvYmnRBVQzXUEw0WlG159yN7lvDyq3uOzt8v9wWUqcQ_cKDlvbqCz56RGZv7vncmjz0htI0ap0YxsU_gtNSzUFrI1QWg_HLTGOyOqNGMaNW6zErOs1nHZLcz-GDOqHCDy7wuG_jlMiPuu1rUdp5Gsu83peWqlJzNfLpM6xJwM1oG2KWCYZIYiGs7fVcqnHDPr_rAdvL1GLNNzLc-mF-Dvg76uufaKMDH6j0Y9DXVTAFeIlHn2l7imbCIQEHAgY08lC46Q2NxuzNTieKoAbPgBlS62DDXp_CpUecDuuCG2N8sc73z4Xq9N4-D-e0TcLzxf6aJtcaM7kGxvFuOzYbbk3KmvqzDJOMBlD_-_KPoD6v_BQAA__9DPpx7) or running around a platform in the basement, it might feel like they vanished almost overnight. But the story of what happened to toy trains involves a slow climb, a long peak, and then a gentle fade into niche territory. The tale really starts in 1900, when Joshua Lionel Cowen built an electric train to sit in a store window and draw shoppers’ eyes. That little display kicked off a new kind of toy: electric trains running on metal track, expandable and endlessly tweakable. By the 1940s and 1950s, sales were surging, especially in the United States. Big department stores set up elaborate layouts at Christmastime, and [ads for train sets](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1uwjAUxfGncTYif8Z48FCJstGpUsfIjm_wRQYj54LF21f0FbqeI_2G_89pPgUseDt_hSt4Wut8g74VIII2Xx4b4QJbhtAiNsoNz5mWHGbJpdkJueN2TgHLa9ZD8iuABDWAF3Yyghuj7JD9PsUUjQtaGy0mpxNwt-5lsk7w6JId0L81IbkVghtlRqftlCAqyVNYuQhMc0hItY2XijfKsJZXfUIbl3odis9E942pDyaPTB577-OrPugR4f2_l0BLZur4ZOpw6J_OyG844GloPt07loLbZJnm52vA8kfSvzo8vfwNAAD___wpcLM) were everywhere—in catalogs, comic books, and early television. The image of a boy kneeling beside his new Lionel layout became such a staple that it turned into a kind of cultural shorthand for mid-century childhood. However, in the 1960s and 1970s, the story quietly shifted course. More serious modelers adopted smaller, more realistic scales, such as HO, transforming the pastime into a detailed craft. For kids, new distractions arrived: slot cars, remote-control toys, video games, and, eventually, home computers. By the 1990s and 2000s, the hobby’s core audience was growing older. Local hobby shops closed, Christmas trees went undecorated by rails, and younger generations often encountered trains only in malls or holiday movies like _The Polar Express_. Yet the hobby hasn’t vanished. It’s simply moved offstage, living on in club layouts, online communities, and high-tech digital controls. The trains still run, but only where you have to go looking for them. _Did you own a model train set—or do you still own one? Let us know by replying to this email._

clean_body: The Supreme Court said Friday it will decide the legality of President Trump's executive order that , which automatically grants citizenship to nearly everyone born in the U.S. | | | | | | Sunday, December 7, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1941, Japan launched a surprise strike on Pearl Harbor, killing about 2,400 Americans and pushing the U.S. into World War II. President Franklin D. Roosevelt called it “a date which will live in infamy.” . Model trains were once a quaint set-up around the Christmas tree, chugging around metal tracks. But their popularity gave way to remote-control toys and more, as explained in today’s If you’ve ever wondered what happens when pure passion for craft beer meets a bold vision for redefining the brewpub experience, today's sponsor, , is the story—and the investment opportunity—you don’t want to miss. | Indiana Beats Ohio State, Duke Upsets Virginia No. 2 Indiana beat No. 1 Ohio State 13-10, after the Buckeyes missed a 27-yard field goal with 2:48 remaining, securing the Hoosiers’ first outright Big Ten title since 1945. In the SEC, No. 3 Georgia beat No. 9 Alabama 28-7, avenging an earlier loss to the Crimson Tide and claiming its second straight conference title. No. 4 Texas Tech scored 34 unanswered points in a 34-7 victory over No. 11 BYU, securing the Red Raiders’ first Big 12 title and a likely first-round bye in the College Football Playoff. No. 17 Virginia forced overtime in the ACC championship game with a last-minute touchdown pass, but Duke sealed the 27-20 upset with an interception during the Cavaliers’ overtime possession. The College Football Playoff selection show is set for noon ET today on ESPN. SCOTUS to Rule on Birthright Citizenship The Supreme Court said Friday it will decide the legality of President Trump's executive order that , which automatically grants citizenship to nearly everyone born in the U.S. Under the administration’s plan, those who have at least one parent who is a U.S. citizen or permanent legal resident. The right would not apply to babies born to temporary visitors who entered the country legally or to people who entered the country illegally. Trump signed an on the first day of his second term, but courts so far have blocked its implementation. List: Top Winter Spots for U.S. Travelers A new report ranks cities in New York, Nevada, and Florida in the U.S. for domestic winter vacationers. Data collected from consumers rated destinations on activities, experiences, and budget, identifying New York City, Las Vegas, Orlando, and Key West as top winter spots for those leaving their passports at home. While stateside travelers were split between cold retreats and warm escapes, favored Cancun, Playa del Carmen, Cabo San Lucas, and Bavaro for dependable sunshine and beaches. The study also revealed that London, Bangkok, Cancun, and Paris were the preferred destinations for non U.S. residents. A new report breaks down the real reason nighttime trips happen — and why it’s especially common after 50. Even better, it shows a simple way to support steady, 24-hour bladder control, cut down those urgent trips, and help avoid leaks and slip-ups. ⇒ | Whatever happened to model trains? If you grew up with a model train or running around a platform in the basement, it might feel like they vanished almost overnight. But the story of what happened to toy trains involves a slow climb, a long peak, and then a gentle fade into niche territory. The tale really starts in 1900, when Joshua Lionel Cowen built an electric train to sit in a store window and draw shoppers’ eyes. That little display kicked off a new kind of toy: electric trains running on metal track, expandable and endlessly tweakable. By the 1940s and 1950s, sales were surging, especially in the United States. Big department stores set up elaborate layouts at Christmastime, and were everywhere—in catalogs, comic books, and early television. The image of a boy kneeling beside his new Lionel layout became such a staple that it turned into a kind of cultural shorthand for mid-century childhood. However, in the 1960s and 1970s, the story quietly shifted course. More serious modelers adopted smaller, more realistic scales, such as HO, transforming the pastime into a detailed craft. For kids, new distractions arrived: slot cars, remote-control toys, video games, and, eventually, home computers. By the 1990s and 2000s, the hobby’s core audience was growing older. Local hobby shops closed, Christmas trees went undecorated by rails, and younger generations often encountered trains only in malls or holiday movies like The Polar Express. Yet the hobby hasn’t vanished. It’s simply moved offstage, living on in club layouts, online communities, and high-tech digital controls. The trains still run, but only where you have to go looking for them. Did you own a model train set—or do you still own one? | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]
