He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. We just watched Congress nearly grind the entire system to a halt — and it’s only getting more chaotic.

He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | What happens to your retirement when Washington shuts down again? body:
No. 4 Texas Tech scored 34 unanswered points in a 34-7 victory over No. 11 BYU, securing the [Red Raiders’ first Big 12 title](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLuupDAQRL8GMkZ2Y2wcONhksrvphsiPNrRkHsI9r79fMb9ww1JJR6fq38_046nQNv_1Kzp-533a8FULMuM5MT9etNVAs4S4nFR59dVHpgkEDJ2ETpgpeSqfSbXJBZGsbNFJowcpRitEuzhQgEbmnJKIMqKyInowWYaQMYxSt-QumARhJAjZ21sYrM6jAmVhsD7GRglMxPt54wUZ377m8tmfeN7ivrbFLcxHbfo_DdwbuPvjGnBVVziZYsEG7oHmyzcufj1o3-pCRxc-j-7L6xjj0tW4n9jZFABDyEKYPEbUvcm618kobdIwjNCeLh0vKoWqNo0S8-qpfE34d_89HfwPAAD__xCYg6E) and a likely first-round bye in the College Football Playoff.

## Tech Wins First Big 12 Title
Texas Tech scored 34 unanswered points in a 34-7 victory over Brigham Young University, securing the [Red Raiders’ first Big 12 title](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLuupDAQRL8GMkZ2Y2wcONhksrvphsiPNrRkHsI9r79fMb9ww1JJR6fq38_046nQNv_1Kzp-533a8FULMuM5MT9etNVAs4S4nFR59dVHpgkEDJ2ETpgpeSqfSbXJBZGsbNFJowcpRitEuzhQgEbmnJKIMqKyInowWYaQMYxSt-QumARhJAjZ21sYrM6jAmVhsD7GRglMxPt54wUZ377m8tmfeN7ivrbFLcxHbfo_DdwbuPvjGnBVVziZYsEG7oHmyzcufj1o3-pCRxc-j-7L6xjj0tW4n9jZFABDyEKYPEbUvcm618kobdIwjNCeLh0vKoWqNo0S8-qpfE34d_89HfwPAAD__xCYg6E) and a likely first-round bye in the College Football Playoff. BYU jumped out to a 7-0 lead in the first quarter, but Texas Tech answered with 13 points before halftime and shut out the Cougars 21-0 in the second half. See the complete highlights [here](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGO6jAQheGncToiz8QmuHBxJUTHLXfLaOJMyAiHoHgg8PYr9hW2PUf6iv_73J1Jstwu_2nmqK9x6W68lcyqvHaqj01upZcLYJpWKTpToaTSoUW_A9zZthtI8rtz1RB7OwSoOEK792APwdpqisxpYO_b1jsIDMhAZAMDhUDNwTWVxA8GaFtAC02oex_248GhC-gDpWSc5UF0WWudWPlFZczv5clrnZa5ynFSvRfT_DN4Mnjatq1-Lw999Pz5PwtpmkxzeprmeD1_jUd_nXfztVrjcN8kZyn71jh7mUnyL6l_C_GM-BMAAP__johxNQ). Linebacker Ben Roberts intercepted two passes, and the Red Raiders’ defense produced two more turnovers, holding BYU to a season-low 200 yards. On offense, Texas Tech quarterback Behren Morton threw for 215 yards and two touchdowns. Texas Tech is expected to play in one of the four CFP quarterfinal games on Dec. 31 or Jan. 1.
## PAC Sues Austin Over Parody Logo
Political action committee [Save Austin Now has sued](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2y2yAQRp8GdXhgjaSrgiKNups2pWYFK3knCDywtuy3zzh5hNRn5vs5v76Xb-TEef-JB3l5bWXJdLZEIlQXkcfJua28Wwi3yk0ObBiEFzDQawvajEtETu_FddGvJk62I2_HobfmazKmu3kahzhGQHS9Mw62npBCHPr1ag3FjTr2nzALZrRg7HW6rP00bF8O3AT9hCEoZyiylHqRGwm9sG3pXZ5UL6EcXfI3kXtT1x8KZgXzeZ6X3y_MH6hg_rxRMKcSMCmY8dGEs4J5rZx3zaJL1g2fpP8Rncup24OaDixvfWDGnar-tOk71hLfOpW96EfDnRTMXfXxfnJK3IZRObMfyOnvLvk_m08PfwIAAP__0-aLsQ) Austin City Manager T.C. Broadnax over the PAC’s parody use of the city’s logo. The [lawsuit follows a city attorney’s letter](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkcHOmzAQhJ_G3DayHUjiA4dUFYdWfyu1hx7RYq_BksHUXkJ4-4q-wn-dkWb0zfz56D8wxLCMP3Cmlt8-9QvtJRIz5Z5528NShjAqbaccCs9Y0HLotdQNKA3y3jsM8ejryrWDdEZV1Kr7rVHyYaSsphatGx7e3bz12Cg_4MPVBo0nL01tBluF9gxTWt6VlupqLkNjbv5R69roxqC1opbkAqd84YmY3lh8PNKL8sWmuYrtxLwWcX0K3Qnd7ft-mctyekJ3tMBWhO5OKKG7xBPlU37buJXwIij4IsCtcFhgSTuUjQrYwAfMuOBIGc4mWDEnd0BMYwIXyroxAQdywAnWnFb4Cx5jTBsL3WGG51P9-knfv4lrl2xw4vr1yzky7eU35bXKrVv3EGMot7uo5ThjiP9p-HMfvFr9LwAA__-ZfZ-a) accusing the PAC of using Austin’s logo without authorization in a parody featuring the city’s “A” above the word “audit.” The group seeks a permanent injunction and argues the logo is invalid because the City Council never formally adopted it. The city's logo, [a stylized, wavy blue and green “A,](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGuszAQhE9jOkf2ggEXLv6GLn_7SmTsDaxkTISXJNz-iVzhNdOM5tPM_NzHu6dEef7vV3T8eWxjxndJyIz7yHy8KZeJZg1h2anw6osPTCMoMFKDVN0YPaVzbKroJhWtrtDprjVa9VapanGmi23Uaqq18cb2Uw2-7tG2oQ42NK2pyF0wDarToHRtb5Ox7aNvoLFgrA9BNAoj8bbfeEHGjy-PdG4v3G9hW6vkFuZnEfU_AYOAIZ_PrfBlCRgusoBB2UtaAcNR5LVPwOCPwpTllyeP_EJKRe4YsdCcZSA-ZdrmTU6-LBjldModC0XMfKWr3cXnm1Ki0naiUfPqKX378N9efDn4DQAA___vx4XD)” unveiled in a $1.1 million rebranding project in September, drew immediate backlash. Critics compared it to a math textbook publisher’s logo and Cracker Barrel’s failed rebrand.
## Woman Cuts Fire Hose During Blaze
Dallas police officers arrested a woman early Friday morning for [allegedly stabbing a fire hose](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2OwyAUhE-DOyx4xnYoKLZxl223tMA8YiT8I3gbktuvnCtsO6OZ0Xw_9_luY4r749tuaOgVjnnHWhISYZ6Jfmvci4sPCcuaY6HNFrtQnEFAzyVwMc7exvSeVeONE17LBo0ch16KmxaiWU3fKSeD7HDo_OhQ-oBOKavUgD0or5torjIJYpQgZKdb1-sh3BQoDb22y8KUQB_pyC2tSPiyJaT38cTcLsfWJLMSnYV1XwwmBlOttV1cuV5cPoPpE2EwXRKDyduUbOH12OzOC1lXeIgZ-XoU5D5k7k_PYGqy8WeNKcUyjEyJx2Zj-izS_zg9DfwFAAD__2ZIfPU) while Dallas firefighters were combating a house fire. The blaze broke out just before 1 a.m. on Dec. 5 at a home on Prosperity Avenue near Bonnie View Road in East Oak Cliff. As firefighters battled the blaze, the woman walked up and allegedly stabbed the hose multiple times. Video from the scene shows water shooting from at least six punctures in the line, but crews were still able to extinguish the fire. The suspect, [a 37-year-old Dallas woman](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkT1upzAQR08DnZE_ARcU29Bl2y3RYI__jGQgsidxcvsVmyNsN6MnPenp9-dtewPKdL1-w4kLf6V7u7DVjMxYNuaPRlfd6aV0OApVPqFCYNq01E4oLeS0RaD8vdk-LruMXvW4qGl0Ss5eyv5YtDVzmqPTXqE1u9zBy8lJM0c7SpdUT8sjU1pOSktl_LA7P6bZauu18xBCZyVG4rsMfCDjF9SUv-9PLEO4zz4vB_N77cyvTq-dXltrQ0sAD-z0CoUpZOz0-nR1eg2Fzudt9wmXqAx7FcedsQq6RKKC4rgrCqgiFGxV7MCc8YdEyBnqz12whg8U4b4SlfMx63kSoxt9gMkJ54wS1lgQEL0RZrfezeCTRdmXJb43ypnqOHVWvk6g_K-F_2-Bz0X_DQAA__8PxZg7), was charged with criminal mischief, officials say, and will also be charged with first-degree arson.

clean_body: No. 4 Texas Tech scored 34 unanswered points in a 34-7 victory over No. 11 BYU, securing the and a likely first-round bye in the College Football Playoff. | | | | | | Sunday, December 7, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1948, singer and stage actor was born in Fort Worth. He is best known for his 1983 ballad The Wind Beneath My Wings. He also played the protagonist Jean Valjean in the Broadway production of Les Miserables. Today’s Around Texas section showcases a for Fort Worth and a list of across the metroplex, celebrating the season’s spirit with festive outings. See below for all the details. If you’re proud to call Texas home—or simply captivated by its rich history and bold future— from today's sponsors, the offers a fresh, inspiring way to rediscover the Lone Star State. Abilene ☀️ 57/39° | Amarillo ☁️ 50/32° | Austin ☀️ 68/50° | Brownsville 🌫️️ 86/67° | Corpus Christi 🌫️️ 78/61° | DFW 🌫️️ 57/43° | El Paso ☀️ 63/39° | Houston 🌫️️ 75/56° | Laredo ☁️ 82/58° | Lubbock ☀️ 56/33° | Midland ☀️ 60/44° | San Antonio ☀️ 72/50° | Tyler 🌫️️ 61/39° | Waco ☀️ 62/44° Take The Flyoverwith you. Busy day? Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Tech Wins First Big 12 Title Texas Tech scored 34 unanswered points in a 34-7 victory over Brigham Young University, securing the and a likely first-round bye in the College Football Playoff. BYU jumped out to a 7-0 lead in the first quarter, but Texas Tech answered with 13 points before halftime and shut out the Cougars 21-0 in the second half. See the complete highlights . Linebacker Ben Roberts intercepted two passes, and the Red Raiders’ defense produced two more turnovers, holding BYU to a season-low 200 yards. On offense, Texas Tech quarterback Behren Morton threw for 215 yards and two touchdowns. Texas Tech is expected to play in one of the four CFP quarterfinal games on Dec. 31 or Jan. 1. PAC Sues Austin Over Parody Logo Political action committee Austin City Manager T.C. Broadnax over the PAC’s parody use of the city’s logo. The accusing the PAC of using Austin’s logo without authorization in a parody featuring the city’s “A” above the word “audit.” The group seeks a permanent injunction and argues the logo is invalid because the City Council never formally adopted it. The city's logo, ” unveiled in a $1.1 million rebranding project in September, drew immediate backlash. Critics compared it to a math textbook publisher’s logo and Cracker Barrel’s failed rebrand. Woman Cuts Fire Hose During Blaze Dallas police officers arrested a woman early Friday morning for while Dallas firefighters were combating a house fire. The blaze broke out just before 1 a.m. on Dec. 5 at a home on Prosperity Avenue near Bonnie View Road in East Oak Cliff. As firefighters battled the blaze, the woman walked up and allegedly stabbed the hose multiple times. Video from the scene shows water shooting from at least six punctures in the line, but crews were still able to extinguish the fire. The suspect, , was charged with criminal mischief, officials say, and will also be charged with first-degree arson. | Flying together with our sponsor The has officially landed! Now the official State Book of Texas, the 73rd edition celebrates a new designation with a look and feel that’s out of this world. From the history and future of the Space Age in Texas to the importance of Spanish exploration to the area, and the greatness of the Texas Heritage Trails Program, the featured articles only scratch the surface of what’s inside the Lone Star State’s most popular reference book. This edition also includes a revamped section to highlight all 254 counties, a travel guide, and new photo galleries of Texas stars in music, TV, film, and sports. Plus, there’s hundreds of fresh images, fun “Best of Texas” lists, updated tables, maps, and facts for people of ages to explore. () ➤ San Antonio : The Guadalupe River was named one of Texas’ most snake-populated waterways, home to venomous species including water moccasins, western diamondback rattlesnakes, Texas coral snakes, and broad-banded copperheads. | | Hi Fellow Patriot,

My name is Lauren Toncich -- and for the past seven years, I've helped lead the team behind the curtain at every Turning Point USA event.

Making sure the lights hit at the right moment, the music is pumping through the crowd, and tens of thousands of people walk into something unforgettable.

I've never emailed you directly before.

But if you've ever seen the opening night of AmericaFest... felt the electricity of a Student Action Summit crowd... or watched one of our massive rallies ignite a room...

Then you've witnessed the kind of energy Charlie dreamed of -- brought to life by our incredible events team.
---
[](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=67922fa31d0f409babd7f96b5a429025&_e=DtGfDzj2SGAddKPg6fp6KzB25oJOuF7TYOA1-351-vsOXufQwDzvEyrP2rgc2iZfU8VQu7TncN9qYceDgvAOsL2lfR6vwZwVz05bAmfPkRc293jG_zdosEbrGgY7lfcGwibmqJGCLXLW3jiEue_n_Hm1VK7JJaOSzyXUVBwjQQrK2h-LShz3si5aAGXYOFSce4kkfhclaLbfWQZ_tScJEo69_xWHp02MbFJ75deI7GjyutgFyPLqt2VmSz_cKv1oG6oTKLb_QN76L3ye-RH6P77fTPopTZlG1H2WhKZvDP0T3Ytc-o7QQcUZGbeakOKLAk9ALxaz0JMXW0CUcNK_Hs1l_e04NJYWsn97kVr4ILIb4kTZKLI78PPhX6Va7_eTCm4CtlLB-SZJln-27ZWZlyOlXOtmWoe6hhgUOVNqaMfo4tx4hMGOq3kn5ZgTzCIpk1uXpyqJIW_Yeflwdk5SfJHBP7tJOVXdMrzGbo1RokjWQE6SDbzqGI9oA_a1OASmyb1NZK-1CF6RoBgRuZ2oiQUI5TziOWFtU_stADRpTGLEQGPfkfVsltCxhd9hKjYtx4C3mvuWa80X3fzQsgDTVU8FKDYNBuZL1aZqxsYoqLTBk_2NldD_cj-oFckXO1yY8nvtw1cmswteSKU4RwMltvdwqNrgAD6g5Olv_6HaFBTyZ5yRf51uo6sLMtIjuFI-)

_Above: That 's me with Charlie, backstage at last year's AmFest making sure every detail was perfect before showtime. _
---
At the heart of every single Turning Point event is this one truth: _Young Americans deserve a stage worthy of the fight they 're in. _

**And AmericaFest -- especially this year -- is our most important stage of all. **

**This December 18 -21 in Phoenix, thousands of students will walk into the most powerful conservative gathering in America. **

It will be our first AmFest without Charlie.

This is the stage he built -- with vision, grit, and sky-high standards.

Charlie poured everything into these events, because he believed students deserved more than beige ballrooms and lazy lectures.

He believed they needed to _FEEL_ the mission -- That they were part of something unstoppable... something big enough to change the world.

Backstage, I can still hear him directing the show: _" Louder music... more sparks... bigger energy... how full are we looking?" _

Not because he wanted flash for the sake of flash...

But because he knew energy moves people. Environments shape conviction.

_And atmosphere matters when you 're calling students into the fight of their lives. _

**And we only have a few days left to get them in. **

clean_body: The one event Charlie loved most...| | | | | Hi Fellow Patriot, My name is Lauren Toncich -- and for the past seven years, I've helped lead the team behind the curtain at every Turning Point USA event. Making sure the lights hit at the right moment, the music is pumping through the crowd, and tens of thousands of people walk into something unforgettable. I've never emailed you directly before. But if you've ever seen the opening night of AmericaFest... felt the electricity of a Student Action Summit crowd... or watched one of our massive rallies ignite a room... Then you've witnessed the kind of energy Charlie dreamed of -- brought to life by our incredible events team. - Above: That 's me with Charlie, backstage at last year's AmFest making sure every detail was perfect before showtime. At the heart of every single Turning Point event is this one truth: Young Americans deserve a stage worthy of the fight they 're in. And AmericaFest -- especially this year -- is our most important stage of all. This December 18 -21 in Phoenix, thousands of students will walk into the most powerful conservative gathering in America. It will be our first AmFest without Charlie. This is the stage he built -- with vision, grit, and sky-high standards. Charlie poured everything into these events, because he believed students deserved more than beige ballrooms and lazy lectures. He believed they needed to FEEL the mission -- That they were part of something unstoppable... something big enough to change the world. Backstage, I can still hear him directing the show: " Louder music... more sparks... bigger energy... how full are we looking?" Not because he wanted flash for the sake of flash... But because he knew energy moves people. Environments shape conviction. And atmosphere matters when you 're calling students into the fight of their lives. This year's AmFest? I 've never seen more students eager to be part of it. Nearly 6,000 are registered. Another 2,200 are on the waitlist. And we only have a few days left to get them in. Fellow Patriot, these students aren't looking for entertainment... They're looking for courage, purpose, and a movement to belong to. They're stepping onto hostile campuses. They're getting flak from Leftist professors. Many are leading Turning Point chapters in places where they're told to sit down, shut up -- or worse. They want to stand for America... They just need a chance to stand with others who will, too. And when they get to AmFest, it's not just the main stage that changes their lives. Behind the lights and cameras, they sit in smaller breakout rooms -- face-to-face with staff and speakers who show them exactly how to lead. They learn how to organize. How to launch and grow chapters. How to push back against the Left -- strategically and fearlessly. They're trained to fight -- and win. Because inspiration is just the beginning. We want every student walking out of AmFest equipped for the battlefield they're stepping into. Fellow Patriot, that 's where you can make a difference. Every $50 you give today helps sponsor a student to attend AmericaFest. And today, your gift will be MATCHED -- dollar-for-dollar. That means: 🔥 $50 sends TWO students 🔥 $100 sends FOUR 🔥 $250 sends EIGHT, and more. Every year, I see it firsthand: Students walk in with doubts, with fears, with barely enough money to afford the flight. They walk out with confidence, connection, and a new sense of calling. I've seen them go home and start chapters. I've seen them run for office. Many tell me -- even years later, that this one event changed everything. So no -- I'm not just asking you to sponsor an "event." I 'm asking you to help change the trajectory of a young patriot. Fellow Patriot, I know what Charlie would want right now. He'd want every seat filled. He'd want AmFest to echo with boldness and fire. He'd want us to fight like he's still backstage -- still calling for more sparks. Most of all, he'd want every student who made the trip to believe they were part of something bigger than themselves. To walk in searching for purpose -- and leave filled with it. . Let's make sure every young patriot who wants to fight for this country gets their chance -- and gets to Phoenix this December. | | For Charlie, Lauren Toncich Vice President of Events Turning Point USA P.S. This is our first AmericaFest without Charlie. But it will be the most important one we've ever hosted. Help us fill the room he loved -- with students ready to carry his mission forward. 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: Neil Armstrong reported a strange encounter he had in space during a post-mission debrief.| | | | December 07 | | | | | Neil Armstrong’s SHOCKING Apollo 11 confession | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Get the full truth now while it’s still publicly available. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. subject: You’ll love my cinnamon roll recipe!
body:
|
#
Hi Friend, It’s that time of year when everything seems to speed up. Between the family gatherings, the prep, and the (joyful!) chaos, there’s so much to do! But every year, one of my favorite traditions is baking homemade cinnamon rolls to ring in the holidays. I always look forward to sharing them with my family, because I know how much they appreciate them. I am so grateful you’ve stuck with me during this campaign, and as a thank you, I want to share my special recipe with you. Join our coalition today by making a contribution, and I’ll send you instructions for how to make this sweet treat – perfect for a Michigan morning or a holiday weekend. name: Marc Elias
subject: re: Republican extremism
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Democratic Association of Secretaries of State, Treasurer Denise Merrill
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120783905/587354643/-248329304?refcode=em-12.7.25-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiOGY2YjViOS03NWQzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=gVBorQxzma4xLFc0ZPxbsmaqG-oYQnTQmc6_GIchskg=) Hi there, it’s Marc Elias. I’m proud to be one of the leading lawyers in the ongoing fight to defend democracy and protect voting rights in the face of Republican extremism. Right now, we’re seeing Donald Trump and his cronies launch an assault on our government and the rule of law. They’re spreading dangerous lies, attacking our institutions, and working to undermine our democracy for _their_ own gain. **But here’s the truth: We’re not going to let them get away with it.** My friends at the Democratic Association of Secretaries of State are standing up to these ongoing threats, but they can’t do this alone. They need Democrats like you in this fight, friend. That’s why I’m asking you to make a donation to DASS today. name: Mark Kelly
subject: The Department of Defense is coming after me.
body:
You can bet this was ordered by Donald Trump and carried out by Pete Hegseth. But right now, that's besides the point.
The Department of War is coming after me because they don’t like what I have to say.
[](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251207-1.-X-l1R&refcode=em-20251207-1&t=1&akid=1023%2E1086043%2Ex0yCZ3)
When I was 22 years old, I swore an oath to the Constitution. I've upheld that oath every day since.
If Trump is trying to intimidate me from doing my job, it won’t work.
I’ve given too much to this country to be silenced by bullies who care more about their own power than about protecting the Constitution.
Call this what it is: **Political persecution.**
You can bet this was ordered by Donald Trump and carried out by Pete Hegseth. But right now, that's besides the point.
**[The point is that I need you to chip in whatever you can afford to Wes Moore and me so we have the resources necessary to fight back against what comes next.](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251207-1.-X-l1R&refcode=em-20251207-1&t=2&akid=1023%2E1086043%2Ex0yCZ3)**

We will not give up the ship.
Mark Kelly

name: VoteVets
subject: Exonerated? Bullsh*t!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
The White House and Hegseth’s spokesperson say that he was totally exonerated by the Department of Defense Inspector General’s report into Signalgate.
#
Peter —
The White House and Hegseth’s spokesperson say that he was totally exonerated by the Department of Defense Inspector General’s report into Signalgate.
Yes, the very same report that found Hegseth violated regulations and endangered the lives of the men and women in harm’s way on that mission.
**This is nuclear-grade gaslighting. If any one of us did the same as Hegseth we would be on trial and on our way to severe punishment.**
Trump and the GOP want to brush this under the rug and act like there’s nothing to see here. They want everyone to think the book is closed.
Bullsh*t. We decide when this is over, and we’re not going to let Hegseth have a single day of peace until he’s run out of the Department of Defense with his tail between his legs.
But we can’t make that kind of noise and get those kinds of results on our own. We need you with us, and we need you speaking out.
[**Please, add your name to this petition and demand Hegseth’s resignation. And once you’ve done that, share it with your friends. We want thousands of people — Veterans, Military families, and everybody in between — speaking out on this.**](https://act.votevets.org/signup/251203_vv_em_hegseth/?refcode=em251207-1-160&akid=34807%2E3013755%2ElnkG7l)
| [ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.votevets.org/signup/251203_vv_em_hegseth/?refcode=em251207-1-160&akid=34807%2E3013755%2ElnkG7l)
---
We know the odds seem high here. But the only thing that has ever changed anything for the better is a ton of righteously pissed people coming together and doing something about it.
Join us.

clean_body: The White House and Hegseth’s spokesperson say that he was totally exonerated by the Department of Defense Inspector General’s report into Signalgate. Peter — The White House and Hegseth’s spokesperson say that he was totally exonerated by the Department of Defense Inspector General’s report into Signalgate. Yes, the very same report that found Hegseth violated regulations and endangered the lives of the men and women in harm’s way on that mission. This is nuclear-grade gaslighting. If any one of us did the same as Hegseth we would be on trial and on our way to severe punishment. Trump and the GOP want to brush this under the rug and act like there’s nothing to see here. They want everyone to think the book is closed. Bullsh*t. We decide when this is over, and we’re not going to let Hegseth have a single day of peace until he’s run out of the Department of Defense with his tail between his legs. But we can’t make that kind of noise and get those kinds of results on our own. We need you with us, and we need you speaking out. | We know the odds seem high here. But the only thing that has ever changed anything for the better is a ton of righteously pissed people coming together and doing something about it. Join us. name: Ilhan Omar for Congress
subject: “Ilhan Omar is garbage.”
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251207-io.09qqW-&refcode=em20251207-37256&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=37256%2E1203867%2EmZ183E ]Tweet by Aaron Rupar: Trump on Somalis: “We’re gonna go the wrong way
if we keep taking in garbage into our country. Ilhan Omar is garbage.
She’s garbage. Her friends are garbage.”

Peter — Trump just can’t stop
attacking Ilhan.

First, Trump spent months going after Ilhan with racist conspiracies,
leading to ongoing harassment and constant threats to Ilhan’s life and
requiring us to hire our own security.

Then, Trump’s rhetoric over the last weeks has put a target on the backs
of innocent Somali-American families here in Minneapolis who already face
so much hate from the far-right.

Now, Trump is calling Ilhan “garbage” and adding that immigrants like her
are “garbage” too. It’s disgusting and dangerous.

And more so, it’s a distraction from the fact that Trump’s first year in
office has been a disaster. The cost of living continues to rise, millions
are losing their healthcare, and ICE has waged a terror campaign in our
cities. The American people are waking up to Trump’s incompetence, and he
is desperate to create a distraction.

So, Peter, we are reaching out
today to ask this grassroots team to help us show that thousands, or even
millions, of people stand with Ilhan Omar as she faces down Trump’s
attacks and continues to fight for our agenda of healthcare, housing,
education, and a safe and affordable life for all Americans, regardless of
where they were born.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251207-io.09qqW-&refcode=em20251207-37256&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=37256%2E1203867%2EmZ183E ]Split a contribution of $5 or whatever you can afford
between Ilhan Omar’s campaign and Justice Democrats today. We want to
prove that this movement has Ilhan’s back and is ready to meet these
racist, hateful attacks with a powerful grassroots campaign that can
defeat Trump and the far-right.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251207-io.09qqW-&refcode=em20251207-37256&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1022&akid=37256%2E1203867%2EmZ183E ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Ilhan Omar for Congress

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20251207-37256-footer&t=1023&refcode2=37256_1203867_mZ183E&akid=37256%2E1203867%2EmZ183E

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. | Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23692%2E4449941%2EgSS6rs) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23692%2E4449941%2EgSS6rs) [**Hey → We’re inviting Top Democrats across the country to participate in our Joe Biden Legacy Survey, reflecting on his achievements, leadership, and impact on the nation!**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70532?t=3&akid=23692%2E4449941%2EgSS6rs) [](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70532?t=4&akid=23692%2E4449941%2EgSS6rs) [ **Team: Please begin your Joe Biden Legacy survey now to represent Democrats in your city →**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70532?t=5&akid=23692%2E4449941%2EgSS6rs) | [BEGIN MY JOE BIDEN LEGACY SURVEY →](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70532?t=6&akid=23692%2E4449941%2EgSS6rs)
---
[ ****1,099 more local responses needed before 11:59 PM to validate our Democratic data ****](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70532?t=7&akid=23692%2E4449941%2EgSS6rs)
Team, President Joe Biden showed us what a TRUE leader looks like.

Remember, during his time as President, Joe Biden:

\- **ADVANCED** voting rights legislation,
\- **NOMINATED** the first Black woman to the Supreme Court,
\- **HELPED** our country recover and rebuild after the COVID-19 pandemic and
\- **WORKED** to restore stability and integrity to the White House.

_And that’s just to name a few of his accomplishments!!_

As we honor his legacy, we’re gathering nationwide feedback on how Americans view the progress made during his administration.

Please – your input will help us understand how Democrats in your city feel about President Joe Biden’s accomplishments and legacy.
[ **Please, begin your President Joe Biden Legacy Survey to represent Top Democrats in your city! Your response is absolutely crucial to validating our local data → →**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70532?t=8&akid=23692%2E4449941%2EgSS6rs)
[BEGIN MY JOE BIDEN LEGACY SURVEY →](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/70532?t=9&akid=23692%2E4449941%2EgSS6rs)

Help her defend Michigan from MAGA Mike

null,

Democrats can win in Michigan, but we need to stand with Haley Stevens. Here’s why:
**FIRST** – early polling showed Haley as the best Democrat to defeat Trump-endorsed Republican Mike Rogers in the general election.
**THEN** – more polling came out showing Haley in a _statistical tie_ with Rogers.
**NOW** – brand-new polling shows Haley leading the Democratic primary – and the experts are saying she is **the _only_ Democrat with a chance of winning.**
The bad news is that _The Hill_ just ranked Michigan as one of the most likely Senate seats to flip. But if we all put our grassroots support behind Haley, there is no question she can pull ahead of MAGA Mike in the polls and defeat him on Election Day.
**That’s why we’ve set a $75,000 weekly fundraising goal to enable us to reach more voters and get the word out about Haley’s campaign – but we need your help to reach it.**[**Will you chip in $5 or more right now to help Haley take the lead in her race, keep Michigan blue, and flip the Senate?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10090?t=4&akid=1574%2E49239%2EmOkEQw)

Team Haley

* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. Haley

name: Jon Ossoff for Georgia
subject: Absolutely pummeled...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Off the Sidelines PAC
body:
|
Friends, As you read this, Jon Ossoff is getting absolutely pummeled by attack ads in Georgia. Republicans have spent more than _$10 million_ on TV and online already. It’s ridiculous. Why so much money, this far from Election Day? Because Jon Ossoff is the only Democratic Senator running for reelection in a state won by Trump. _That_ is why Republicans are hitting him so hard, so early in the campaign. _And it’s why we’re coming to you with this request to help fight back._ If Jon is going to defend this seat, we _really_ need to keep hitting our fundraising goals, and donations to emails like this are a huge part of how it happens. **[_Can Jon Ossoff count on you to split $10, $50 or whatever you can afford to help him lay the foundation for a winning Senate reelection campaign here in Georgia? Your donation will be split between his reelection campaign and Off the Sidelines._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120792041/587379901/-116537968?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.07_OTS-Jon-Ossoff_B2_F1_S1_C1_90AC-NC_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNDYxNWY0Yy03YmQzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ZfNvVBorJX2gqB6Ag0n2ajfHVk7tMIK-AadKV1C8_0E=)** | | [ DONATE NOW >> ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/120792042/587379902/-116537968?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.07_OTS-Jon-Ossoff_B2_F1_S1_C1_90AC-NC_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNDYxNWY0Yy03YmQzLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ZfNvVBorJX2gqB6Ag0n2ajfHVk7tMIK-AadKV1C8_0E=)
---
We wish we didn’t have to ask at all, but _Citizens United_ opened the dam for limitless dark money to flood into our elections.
_That’s a big part of why Congress is broken and corrupted. It’s why we have really expensive elections._
And also it’s why campaigns like ours have to build serious grassroots fundraising operations _from the very start._
Because if we don’t, we run the risk of our message getting drowned out by a flood of Super PAC spending.
So here’s the truth:
**Jon needs our help _today_.**
_He is literally MAGA’s #1 target._
They will spare no expense and try every dirty trick in the book.
So yes, this election will be tough.
But this team knows how to win tough fights. We’ve done it before.
Let’s do it again.

clean_body: | Friends, As you read this, Jon Ossoff is getting absolutely pummeled by attack ads in Georgia. Republicans have spent more than $10 million on TV and online already. It’s ridiculous. Why so much money, this far from Election Day? Because Jon Ossoff is the only Democratic Senator running for reelection in a state won by Trump. That is why Republicans are hitting him so hard, so early in the campaign. And it’s why we’re coming to you with this request to help fight back. If Jon is going to defend this seat, we really need to keep hitting our fundraising goals, and donations to emails like this are a huge part of how it happens. = | | = We wish we didn’t have to ask at all, but Citizens United opened the dam for limitless dark money to flood into our elections. That’s a big part of why Congress is broken and corrupted. It’s why we have really expensive elections. And also it’s why campaigns like ours have to build serious grassroots fundraising operations from the very start. Because if we don’t, we run the risk of our message getting drowned out by a flood of Super PAC spending. So here’s the truth: Jon needs our help today. He is literally MAGA’s #1 target. They will spare no expense and try every dirty trick in the book. So yes, this election will be tough. But this team knows how to win tough fights. We’ve done it before. Let’s do it again. = | = | = Thank you! \- Team Ossoff © 2025 | Paid for by Off the Sidelines PAC

clean_body: Peter, I'm not alone in believing you truly deserve this. My colleagues and I at House GOP Headquarters were all in agreement. In fact, we've made a unanimous decision. If there's anyone who has a record worthy of this, it's you. First of all, Congratulations! This is reserved only for top supporters of our movement. But I have to say, you've been so outstanding in your record of support that you've built up a profile that falls within the TOP Tier of GOP Patriots. one of House GOP's most trusted patriots, and you deserve to have the status to reflect that! And before you click, I want to let you know that we'll be leaning on you for your opinion on matters as we move forward. I'm putting this deal on the table because we can't afford to lose our most trusted allies when our majority is as thin as it is right now! You, God Bless You and God Bless America! Welcome to the weekly roundup. I'm telling you right now, this one _hits different_.
Because this week, Blue Amp Media didn't just cover the news... we **broke** it.
[Melissa Corrigan, she/her](https://open.substack.com/users/13839118-melissa-corrigan-sheher?utm_source=mentions) and [David Shuster](https://open.substack.com/users/16891317-david-shuster?utm_source=mentions) uncovered documents proving the U.S. Navy ordered sailors to **delete photo and video evidence** after their own ship was bombed by U.S. forces. That's not a story, that's a five-alarm scandal -- and it's the first of many we're going to drag into the light.
This is what we're building here. Real journalism. Real accountability.
And we're just getting started.

name: Deb Haaland
subject: These moments shaped who I am
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
Every family deserves to have a fair shot.
Hey Peter — it's Deb Haaland. When I think about the holiday season, I think about the parents who are putting on a brave face for their children while they try to make ends meet. I know that feeling well.
About fifteen years ago, during this same time, my child and I were struggling. The only food in our cupboard was pinto beans and flour, and I wanted to give them a real holiday meal. I applied for emergency food stamps, but I was denied. I broke down in tears because I didn’t know how to tell my child: It’s expensive to be poor.
That night, we ate what we had, and I tried to keep their spirits up. **This year, many families are doing the same thing, trying to stay strong for their kids even when things feel uncertain. It shouldn’t be this hard.**
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98474?refcode=em20251207-9803&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9803%2E614464%2ElpMU_A)
Peter, these moments shaped who I am. It’s why I strongly believe leaders should make people’s lives easier, not harder. We all want good roads, safe communities, strong education, access to parks and affordable, healthy food to eat.
**[This campaign is about giving every family in New Mexico the fair shot they deserve. If you’re able, will you split a donation of $10, or anything you can, between Heartland Patriots and our campaign to help us keep New Mexico moving forward?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98475?refcode=em20251207-9803&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=10&t=2&akid=9803%2E614464%2ElpMU_A)**
**[DONATE](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98474?refcode=em20251207-9803&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=9803%2E614464%2ElpMU_A)**
I know the fights we face today are bigger than ever – and I’m prepared to get to work immediately. On Day One, I will begin tackling the rising affordability crisis and healthcare costs, so we can build a state where every single person is safe, healthy, and can thrive. But I can’t do this work alone.
Our grassroots-powered campaign was built for the people, by the people – and that includes you, Peter. **[So please, can I count on you to split $10, $25, or whatever you can comfortably spare between my campaign and Heartland Patriots to help fuel our grassroots movement, win in New Mexico, and build a better future for working families?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98474?refcode=em20251207-9803&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=9803%2E614464%2ElpMU_A)**

clean_body: Every family deserves to have a fair shot. Hey Peter — it's Deb Haaland. When I think about the holiday season, I think about the parents who are putting on a brave face for their children while they try to make ends meet. I know that feeling well. About fifteen years ago, during this same time, my child and I were struggling. The only food in our cupboard was pinto beans and flour, and I wanted to give them a real holiday meal. I applied for emergency food stamps, but I was denied. I broke down in tears because I didn’t know how to tell my child: It’s expensive to be poor. That night, we ate what we had, and I tried to keep their spirits up. This year, many families are doing the same thing, trying to stay strong for their kids even when things feel uncertain. It shouldn’t be this hard. Peter, these moments shaped who I am. It’s why I strongly believe leaders should make people’s lives easier, not harder. We all want good roads, safe communities, strong education, access to parks and affordable, healthy food to eat. I know the fights we face today are bigger than ever – and I’m prepared to get to work immediately. On Day One, I will begin tackling the rising affordability crisis and healthcare costs, so we can build a state where every single person is safe, healthy, and can thrive. But I can’t do this work alone. Our grassroots-powered campaign was built for the people, by the people – and that includes you, Peter. This is Donald Trump Jr.

Fellow American, we have a serious problem. The NRSC is building the most accurate Republican voter file before the 2025 elections - _but your record is incomplete_.

We've already verified 1,482 voters in your area.

name: NPR + PBS Alert via Our Revolution
subject: Add your name: Save NPR and PBS
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ]Our Revolution Logo

Peter Willis,

Public media stations across the country are shutting down, victims of
historic defunding by Trump and Congress.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34850?source=email-20251207&t=1011&akid=9943%2E5885482%2ET7Tqrm ]Headline: The Defunding of Public Media is Hitting Local Stations
Hardest

KWSU-TV, one of the oldest public-media stations in the Pacific Northwest,
and NJ PBS, New Jersey's only public-television station, announced plans
to close their doors. And unless we act, they won't be the last. 115+
public radio and TV stations nationwide are at risk of shutting down if we
don't do something – and fast.

Right now, the Senate is working on its 2026 budget and can RESTORE
funding for public media. But it's going to take a MASSIVE public outcry
to force them to do it.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34850?source=email-20251207&t=1012&akid=9943%2E5885482%2ET7Tqrm ]Click here to email your lawmakers NOW and demand they include funding
for public media in the 2026 appropriations process!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34850?source=email-20251207&t=1013&akid=9943%2E5885482%2ET7Tqrm ]ADD YOUR NAME

Public media is a community lifeline – built for everyone, regardless of
zip code, wallet size, or background. For millions of Americans –
especially in rural and Tribal areas – these are the only local outlets
for news, learning, and emergency alerts.

But as Trump looks to hand full control of the media to his oligarch
friends, he slashed funding for NPR and PBS – forcing layoffs, killing
local shows, and risking total shutdowns where stations can't survive
without federal help.

It's not too late to reverse course, but Congress is racing to finish the
budget by January. Which gives us just weeks to flood Congress with our
demands. Our Revolution is ready to deliver our voices straight to
Congress in these crucial moments, but first we need to come together in
massive numbers to show that our public outrage cannot be ignored.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34850?source=email-20251207&t=1014&akid=9943%2E5885482%2ET7Tqrm ]We CANNOT let Trump and his oligarchs get away with silencing our
public media for their own gain. ACT NOW and tell Congress to restore
public media funding in the 2026 budget before more communities are left
in the dark.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34850?source=email-20251207&t=1015&akid=9943%2E5885482%2ET7Tqrm ]ADD YOUR NAME

When we organize, we win.

Our Revolution

Our Revolution is the largest entirely grassroots funded progressive
group in America.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/26880?c0=email-20251207.UXPvTg&t=1008&akid=9943%2E5885482%2ET7Tqrm ]Help us defend democracy and take the fight directly to the
establishment by committing to be a Movement Builder with a recurring

Peter,
I wanted to make sure you saw Tim’s message about who his newest opponent is:
Mike Lindell is Donald Trump’s pick for Governor and we have to be ready to stop him.
**[Will you chip in $5 to fight back against MAGA Mike Lindell?](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251207.fkwWxk&amount=5&refcode=em20251207-247&t=2&akid=247%2E34582%2Eg_dl6X)**
